rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM ROY HORSTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Excusing Respondent Lidstrom From Answering Interrogatories, Producing Documents And Admitting Facts, And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: The motion for protective order excusing respondent Lidstrom from Answering Interrogatories, Producing Documents and Admitting Facts, and for Sanctions is denied. Given the Court's denial of the jurisdiction challenge to the Fee Petition, the Respondent must respond to the requested discovery. Petitioner's Request for Sanctions is denied. The prevailing party must prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest must notify the court and opposing counsel no later than 3/1/2016, at 4:00 p.m. If a hearing is needed, it will be scheduled for 3/16/2016, at 2:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM ROY HORSTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Petitioner Alexandra Ward; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion; Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Ciaran O'Sullivan
rulings.Rulings: Respondent Lasse Lidstrom's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is DENIED. Petitioner Alexandra Ward has alleged sufficient facts regarding her legal services provided on appeal to Respondent / the Horstman Estate to state a cause of action for her fees pursuant to Probate Code Section 10832. (Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 7.702.) Probate Code Section 10832(c) allows an attorney for the personal representatitve to petition the Court for extraordinary compensation for good cause shown. Petitioner has made claer that she seeks remuneration pursuant to the Probate Code and not the existing Contingency Fee Agreement under which Albertson & Davidson (A&D) also seeks a Court Order for payment of its fees for work performed. Further, this Court has jurisdiction over the subject of the cause of action alleged in the Petition filed by A&D to Approve Extraordinary Fees to Alexandra Ward. Petitioner's alleged facts as to her services give her standing under section 10832 because she provided extraodinary services for which she is entitled to request subject to the Court's discretion. (Prob. Code Section 10811.) The prevailing party shall submit a proposed order. If any party chooses to contest the tentative, they must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. on 3/1/2016. Any hearing would need to be scheduled for 3/16, at 2:00 p.m.
